name: Mercury
summary: Mercury is the smallest and innermost planet. With almost no atmosphere it experiences extreme day/night temperature swings. Its surface is heavily cratered and preserves a record of early Solar System impacts, including the large Caloris Basin.

name: Venus
summary: Venus is similar in size to Earth but has a crushing carbon-dioxide atmosphere and sulfuric-acid clouds. A runaway greenhouse effect produces surface temperatures hot enough to melt lead; surface features (volcanic plains, large shield volcanoes) have been revealed by radar.

name: Earth
summary: Earth is the third planet and the only known world that supports life. Roughly 71% of its surface is covered with water. Its atmosphere, magnetic field and active geology create a stable environment for diverse ecosystems.

name: Mars
summary: Mars, the Red Planet, has a cold, dry surface covered in iron-oxide dust. Evidence of ancient river channels and lakebeds shows it once had liquid water on the surface. It hosts the largest volcano and the deepest canyon in the Solar System.

name: Jupiter
summary: Jupiter is the largest planet and a gas giant of hydrogen and helium. Its strong gravity sculpts the outer Solar System, and its magnetosphere and storms (notably the Great Red Spot) dominate its appearance. It hosts many moons, several with astrobiological interest.

name: Saturn
summary: Saturn is a gas giant best known for its spectacular ring system made of ice and rock. It has dozens of moons including Titan and Enceladus, which are high-priority targets for astrobiology.

name: Uranus
summary: Uranus is an ice giant with a pale blue color from methane; it rotates on its side, likely due to a giant collision early in its history. Its ring system and moons are icy and highly interesting for interior/formation studies.

name: Neptune
summary: Neptune is an ice giant with very strong winds and weather patterns. Its deep blue color is caused by methane and other atmospheric components. Triton, its largest moon, has a retrograde orbit indicating capture.
